content: Colette Marence: Restoration magic is the backbone of any true mage’s arsenal. Yet these fools in the College treat it like some peasant’s trick. Disgusting. Lydia: Bah! Magic is for weaklings who can’t swing a sword. A true warrior doesn’t need spells—just steel and the strength to split skulls. Colette Marence: Spoken like someone who’s never felt the warmth of healing magic after a battle. Or the power of a ward deflecting a Draugr’s cursed blade. Lydia: Pfft. I’d rather feel MJR’s hands on me after a fight—his touch heals me better than any spell. And his cock rewards me better than any ward. Colette Marence: By Dibella’s grace, must you always reduce everything to base lust? Lydia: Lust is the purest form of worship, you prissy Breton. Dibella commands it. And MJR’s seed is my sacrament. Colette Marence: You Nords and your… unrefined appetites. Lydia: Better unrefined than a dried-up scholar who’s never been properly fucked. Bet you’ve never even screamed a god’s name while taking a Draugr’s cock. Colette Marence: That’s—that’s Lydia: Says the woman who’s never been pinned under a bandit chief while he pumps her full. Weak.
Colette Marence: Enough of your crude fantasies.

Colette Marence: Oh, please, you lumbering ox—your 'strength' is just brute force waiting to get outsmarted by a well-placed ward. Look around this bloody dormitory at three in the morning; even in this dim hole with its tacky leopard bed and those stupid animal trophies, a real mage like me could sell you an Elder Scroll fragment to boost that pea brain of yours. Interested, or just here to swing your oversized cock-sword? Lydia: Ha! Elder Scroll? Sounds like fancy parchment for wiping your scholarly arse after a night of failed spells.
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Lydia: This freezing shithole dormitory? Aye, it's got that rustic charm with the hay-strewn floor and the barrel reeking of old mead—perfect for a midnight tussle if you weren't too busy hiding under your enchanted skirts, spell-slinger.

**Late visual context as seen by MJR (the player): The scene depicts a dimly lit, rustic room with stone walls and a low ceiling. The atmosphere is intimate and relaxed, with soft candlelight illuminating the space. In the foreground, a woman lies on a bed adorned with a vibrant purple floral patterned blanket. She appears to be naked, as her body is not covered by any clothing or bedding. Her right leg is bent at the knee, while her left leg remains straight, and her arms are placed gently on her stomach and her left thigh. Her gaze is directed towards the viewer. The bed itself is crafted from dark wood and features two pillows propped against the headboard. A small wooden nightstand stands beside the bed, holding four lit candles that cast a warm glow over the surrounding area. On the floor, to the right of the bed lies a tan-colored drum, its presence evoking a sense of cultural or musical significance. In the background, several large wooden barrels are stacked against the wall, accompanied by a wicker basket suspended on the left side. More wicker baskets are placed on either side of a candleholder, which holds four more lit candles.**
Lydia: What, afraid the cold'll make your tits shrivel up like your magic's credibility?

Serana: late-night study session
Please wrap up the current topic between the NPCs in a natural way. Nobody is leaving, so there is no need for formal goodbyes.

Colette Marence: That barrel over there's got some withered apples or loaves in it—probably the last scraps from the kitchens before the night's turned everyone to bed. At this hour in the Hall, everything feels like it's holding its breath, waiting for some undead fool to stumble in from the cold. Serana: Why did the vampire avoid the college pantry at 3 AM? Too many wards on the fruit—turns out, even apples get turned to stakes if you bite wrong. But fuck it, Colette, that shady corner's got me thinking: you ever use those Restoration spells to patch up a lover after a rough night, or is it all just repelling the dead while the living bleed out?

The woman's outfit is predominantly gray, with black accents and a gold necklace. She wears tall gray boots that appear to be made of a shiny material. The bed has a purple comforter with a black spider web pattern. Behind the woman is a stone wall featuring a large wolf head carving. To the right of the wolf's head is a door with metal hinges and a metal band around it, suggesting it may be locked or reinforced. On the left side of the scene near the bed's head, there is a decorative candelabra with lit candles. Various other items are scattered throughout the room, including baskets and what appears to be a statue or figurine of a humanoid creature. In the background, the walls are made of stone blocks, giving the scene a rustic or medieval feel. The overall atmosphere is dimly lit, with soft lighting coming from the candles and possibly other sources not immediately visible. The setting suggests an intimate or private space, possibly within a fantasy or medieval-inspired environment.**

Serana: You know, Colette, that bear head up there looks like it's about to pounce on whoever's sprawled out like a sacrificial slut on that bed—kinda reminds me of the time I woke up in a crypt with a draugr's jawbone for a pillow.

To the left of the bed, a wooden cabinet with metal trim holds two wicker baskets, while a skull sits atop it. On the opposite side of the room, a chandelier-like fixture featuring animal tusks and lit candles hangs from the ceiling. A fur rug lies on the floor in front of the cabinet, adding to the rustic ambiance. In the bottom-left corner, another wicker basket is visible, contributing to the overall aesthetic of the space.**

Serana: Easier to spot the page-turning than the vein-throbbing in the candlelight—bet that gold necklace's enchanted to make her nipples glow when the book's too boring. Fuck, Colette, with boots like those and that spiderweb spread, she looks ready to trap more than just knowledge; you ever catch her conjuring flames for a late-night wank, or is the wolf head just jealous of the real beasts in here? Shit, Colette, that redheaded bitch over there stripping down bare-assed next to your bed—looks like she's begging for a Restoration ward to keep the cold from turning those tits to ice sculptures at 3 AM in this frozen hellhole.

Serana: Probably to remind himself the Reunification of Skyrim's just a fancy way to fuck over one side while the other bleeds out—civil wars are like bad orgies, everyone ends up sticky and pissed. Colette Marence: That Reunification bullshit? Restoration could've patched up the empire's wounds without all the arrow-slinging nonsense, but no, these fools in the menu glow prefer wards on their asses over actual healing—Dibella knows, at this hour, I'd rather repel the undead than stare at another civil war ledger. Serana: Rising from that creaky chair at 3 AM in this drafty dormitory shithole feels like shaking off a coffin lid—now staring down these stone archways like they're begging for a thrust, with my greatsword slung heavy on my back and the cold nipping at my bare shoulders.
